mechanism_narrative: Sustained positive growth in non-farm payrolls (PAYEMS) and a declining unemployment rate (UNRATE) indicate a robust labor market, which supports consumer spending and corporate earnings, leading to positive S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: SPX 20-day returns do not show a statistically significant positive correlation with periods of sustained PAYEMS growth and UNRATE decline over a 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: When consumer price inflation (CPIAUCSL) exceeds a critical threshold, it signals an overheating economy and prompts central bank tightening, leading to higher discount rates and negative S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: SPX 60-day returns remain positive or flat in more than 60% of instances where CPIAUCSL is above 208.0, or the Rank IC is below 0.05 during such periods.

mechanism_narrative: A combination of strong retail sales (RSAFS) and robust industrial production (INDPRO) indicates broad-based economic strength, suggesting healthy consumer and business demand that drives corporate profitability and positive S&P 500 returns.
candidate_trigger: RSAFS growth > 0.5% MoM AND INDPRO growth > 0.2% MoM
failure_condition: The joint condition of strong RSAFS and INDPRO growth does not lead to statistically significant positive SPX 20-day returns over a 5-year backtest.

mechanism_narrative: During a monetary tightening cycle, further increases in the federal funds rate (FEDFUNDS) signal increasingly restrictive policy, which dampens economic activity and leads to negative S&P 500 forward returns.
candidate_trigger: FEDFUNDS rises by > 0.25% in a month
failure_condition: SPX 60-day returns are positive or flat in more than 50% of instances when FEDFUNDS increases during a tightening cycle.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp decline in housing starts (HOUST) signals weakening consumer and business confidence, indicating a slowdown in future economic activity that translates into negative S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: SPX 5-day returns are not negative following a significant HOUST decline in more than 65% of triggered instances.

mechanism_narrative: Consistent growth in industrial production (INDPRO) reflects healthy manufacturing and supply chain activity, which supports corporate earnings and drives positive S&P 500 forward returns.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO growth > 0.1% MoM for 3 consecutive months
failure_condition: SPX 20-day returns do not show a statistically significant positive trend during periods of sustained INDPRO growth over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A rapid increase in the unemployment rate (UNRATE) coupled with stagnating or declining payrolls (PAYEMS) signals a deteriorating labor market, which reduces consumer spending and corporate profits, leading to negative S&P 500 returns.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE rises by > 0.5 percentage points over 3 months AND PAYEMS growth < 0.1% MoM
failure_condition: SPX 60-day returns are positive or flat in more than 50% of instances despite the combined trigger of rising UNRATE and weak PAYEMS.

mechanism_narrative: Strong real GDP growth (GDPC1) indicates a robust overall economy, which typically translates into higher corporate revenues and profits, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: SPX 60-day returns do not show a statistically significant positive correlation with periods of strong GDPC1 growth over a 10-year backtest.
